title: Register did:plc with Bluesky directory
description:
Silently register a did:plc for each actor on plc.directory. Gives actors Bluesky-compatible portable identity for free.

## Why did:plc

- did:key: no rotation, no recovery
- did:web: dies with server
- did:plc: survives server death, supports rotation, Bluesky-compatible

plc.directory is open — anyone can register, not just Bluesky users.

## did:plc structure

did:plc is a hash of the genesis operation:
```
did:plc:z72i7hdynmk6r22z27h6tvur
```

Genesis operation:
```json
{
  "type": "plc_operation",
  "rotationKeys": ["did:key:z6Mk..."],
  "verificationMethods": {
    "atproto": "did:key:z6Mk..."
  },
  "alsoKnownAs": [
    "at://alice.example.com",
    "https://example.com/users/alice"
  ],
  "services": {
    "atproto_pds": {
      "type": "AtprotoPersonalDataServer",
      "endpoint": "https://example.com"
    }
  },
  "prev": null,
  "sig": "..."
}
```

## Implementation

1. On actor creation, build genesis operation
2. Sign with actor private key
3. POST to https://plc.directory/{did}
4. Store did_plc in actors table
5. Add to actor alsoKnownAs

## API

```
POST https://plc.directory/{did}
Content-Type: application/json

{genesis_operation}
```

The {did} in the URL is derived by hashing the genesis op.

## Migration

Backfill: create did:plc for existing actors (idempotent if we store the genesis op).

## Acceptance

- [ ] New actors get did:plc registered
- [ ] did:plc resolves on plc.directory
- [ ] Rotation keys allow future key updates
- [ ] alsoKnownAs includes AP actor URL
- [ ] Existing actors backfilled
close_reason: Out of scope — Bluesky directory registration is not part of core DID emission
